channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: In battleground Georgia, poor people see no reason to vote. That decision could sway election
item.description: There are more than 47,000 people in Bibb County, Georgia, about 80 miles south of Atlanta, who are eligible to vote but don't.
item.dc:creator: Gary Fields, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Asian American boba brand finds opportunity after Simu Liu sparks cultural appropriation debate
item.description: Olivia Chen and Pauline Ang, friends and business partners on boba milk tea brand Twrl, have tried three times to get on “Shark Tank,” the ABC reality show where up-and-coming entrepreneurs try to woo big-name backers.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Broadway dancer Zelig Williams last seen with headshots: Mom
item.description: Zelig Williams was last seen Oct. 3 carrying professional headshots as he left his house, according to Williams' mom Kathy.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: New campus protest rules spur an outcry from college faculty
item.description: Dissent is thriving this fall at American colleges, and not just among student activists. With student protests limited by new restrictions, faculty have taken up the cause. To faculty, new protest rules threaten freedom of speech — and the freedom to think, both central to university life.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Peru’s ex-president Toledo gets more than 20 years in prison in case linked to corruption scandal
item.description: LIMA, Peru (AP) — Peru’s former President Alejandro Toledo on Monday was sentenced to 20 years and six months in prison in a case involving Brazilian construction giant Odebrecht, which became synonymous with corruption across Latin America, where it paid millions of dollars in bribes to government
item.dc:creator: FRANKLIN BRICEÑO and REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Moldova narrowly votes to secure path toward EU membership after accusing Russia of interference
item.description: CHISINAU, Moldova (AP) — Moldovans voted by a razor-thin majority in favor of securing the country’s path toward European Union membership, electoral data showed Monday, after the pro-Western president accused foreign interference and “criminal groups” of trying to undermine the vote in the former S
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Texas man whose execution was halted doesn't appear at state Capitol after subpoena
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A Texas man whose execution was halted and then called to testify at the state Capitol did not show up Monday over growing pushback from state leaders who have blasted lawmakers' last-ditch tactics to subpoena Robert Roberson and spare his life for now.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge hears arguments to block Louisiana's Ten Commandments displays in schools
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — A federal judge heard arguments at a hearing Monday on whether he should temporarily block a new Louisiana law that requires the Ten Commandments to be displayed in every public school classroom by Jan. 1.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Starbucks releasing 'Wicked' drinks inspired by the upcoming film
item.description: Starbucks is releasing a pair of limited-edition drinks inspired by two main characters of the upcoming film, "Wicked.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, KRON4

item.title: Walmart and Aldi are once again offering meal deals for Thanksgiving
item.description: The two companies are advertising their dinners at "inflation-free" or "pre-inflation" prices, respectively.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, ABC 4

item.title: McDonald's says it doesn't endorse political candidates after Trump visit
item.description: McDonald's is emphasizing its political neutrality after former President Donald Trump spent some time behind the counter at one of its Pennsylvania locations.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, KRON4

item.title: Central Park Five sue Trump for defamation over debate comments
item.description: The exonerated Central Park Five sued former President Donald Trump for defamation on Monday over his comments at the recent presidential debate about their wrongful convictions for rape and assault.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, ABC 4

item.title: Central Park Five sue Donald Trump for jogger case remarks made at presidential debate
item.description: With Election Day two weeks away, the group accused the former president of making “false and defamatory statements.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Writer Olivia Nuzzi, New York magazine part ways after relationship with political source revealed
item.description: New York magazine says the publication and star political reporter Olivia Nuzzi have decided jointly to “part ways.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Footage appears to show moment helicopter crash topples radio tower in Houston
item.description: Footage from the surveillance camera shows an aircraft making contact with something in the distance just after 7:51 p.m., according to the timestamp.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Prosecutors: Ex-officer's gunshots put people in danger the night of deadly Breonna Taylor raid
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — The 10 shots Brett Hankison fired into Breonna Taylor 's apartment the night she was killed in a police raid put Taylor, her neighbors and even one of Hankison’s fellow Louisville police officers in danger, federal prosecutors argued Monday.
item.dc:creator: DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tourists return to NYC's Vessel sculpture after it reopens with netting to prevent deaths
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Tourists once again climbed the steps of Manhattan's beehive-shaped Vessel sculpture after it reopened Monday for the first time in three years, now with netting aimed at decreasing the risk of suicides like the ones that forced its closure.
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO and John Minchillo, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: PHOTOS: White House public tour gets makeover with 'reimagined' visitor experience
item.description: The White House public tour is getting an extreme makeover, with new digital exhibits, an expanded route and more access to rooms poised to be part of the "reimagined" experience for visitors.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walmart and Aldi are once again offering meal deals for Thanksgiving
item.description: The two companies are advertising their dinners at "inflation-free" or "pre-inflation" prices, respectively.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, NewsNation

item.title: Some Jewish voters in presidential swing states reconsider their longtime devotion to Democrats
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — For Rona Kaufman, the signs are everywhere that more Jews feel abandoned by the Democratic Party and may vote for Republican Donald Trump. It's in her Facebook feed.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Democrats urge Biden to push Israel for press freedom in Gaza
item.description: Dozens of House Democrats are pressing the Biden administration to get tougher on Israel when it comes to press freedoms in Gaza, warning that a lack of media access in the war-torn territory has stifled any push for accountability surrounding military operations that have killed tens of thousands o
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Paul Di'Anno, former Iron Maiden lead singer, dies at 66
item.description: Paul Andrews, better known by his stage name, Paul Di'Anno of Iron Maiden fame has died at the age of 66.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: One of the last remaining WWII Navajo Code Talkers dies at 107
item.description: One of the last remaining Navajo Code Talkers who transmitted messages during World War II using the tribe's native language has died, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mormon church now offering sleeveless garments for members in warmer climates
item.description: "Many faithful Latter-day Saints wear a garment under their clothing that has deep religious significance," the Church website states.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Mahon, NewsNation

item.title: What is pink cocaine? Autopsy finds drug in Liam Payne's body
item.description: A partial autopsy conducted on former One Direction singer Liam Payne found multiple substances in his system when he fell to his death from the third-floor balcony of a hotel room in Argentina last week, according to a new report from ABC News.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bird flu outbreak in California kills dairy cattle
item.description: There have been confirmed infections in more than 300 herds of cattle in the U.S. in 14 states since March.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Loo, NewsNation

item.title: Getting from Los Angeles to 2 major European cities just got easier
item.description: Adventurous SoCal residents looking to hop across the pond this summer can do so with ease thanks to one new and one reintroduced nonstop route from a relatively new low-cost airline.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Central Park Five sue Trump for defamation over debate comments
item.description: The exonerated Central Park Five sued former President Donald Trump for defamation on Monday over his comments at the recent presidential debate about their wrongful convictions for rape and assault.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KRON4

item.title: PHOTOS: Giant Pumpkin Regatta makes a splash
item.description: Paddlers dressed in their Halloween best raced in carved-out pumpkins with all their might as part of the West Coast Giant Pumpkin Regatta.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Deml, KRON4

item.title: Who is Lucifer 'Justin Case' Everylove? Meet the presidential candidate who is only on Utah's ballots
item.description: Who is Lucifer "Justin Case" Everylove? Here's how this unaffiliated presidential candidate made it onto the ballots in Utah.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, KRON4

item.title: Harris raises more than $220 million in September, outraising Trump 3-to-1
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris’s campaign raised nearly $222 million in September, raising more than three times as much as former President Donald Trump’s campaign.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Helicopter crashes in Houston’s Second Ward after hitting radio tower, killing 4, officials say
item.description: Officials say four people aboard a tour helicopter were killed when it crashed into a radio tower in Houston and burst into flames.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: One of the last Navajo Code Talkers from World War II dies at 107
item.description: John Kinsel Sr., one of the last remaining Navajo Code Talkers who transmitted messages during World War II based on the tribe's native language, has died. He was 107.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Man's death originally reported as bear attack was actually 'vicious' murder, Montana sheriff says
item.description: A man's death that was previously reported as a bear attack is now being investigated by Montana authorities as a homicide. On Oct. 12, the body of 35-year-old Dustin Kjersem of Belgrade was discovered inside a tent in the wilderness north of Big Sky in Gallatin County.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5
